styles.title: Choose Your Translation Style
styles.sub: Four distinct historical periods — each with its own character. Whether you need an early modern english translator or a middle english translator, we have you covered.
styles.0.name: Shakespeare Style
styles.0.desc: Early Modern English. Thee, thou, dost, hath. Most readable and widely recognised archaic style.
styles.1.name: Medieval Style
styles.1.desc: Chaucer's era. Older spelling and grammar. Ideal for historical fiction and academic work.
styles.2.name: Anglo-Saxon
styles.2.desc: True Old English. Deeply Germanic, most authentic. Used in Beowulf-era literature.
styles.3.name: Archaic Simplified
styles.3.desc: Easy old-timey style. Great for social media, memes, games, and casual use.
guide.title: Old English Translator — Complete Guide
guide.p: Our old english translator is the most advanced free AI tool for converting modern English into authentic archaic language styles spanning over a thousand years of English history. Whether you need an english to old english translator for a creative project, an old english text translator for academic research, or an old english words translator for quick lookups, this tool delivers historically grounded results in seconds.
feat.0.name: Bidirectional Translation
feat.0.desc: Modern to old, or old to modern — works as both an english to old english translator and an old english to modern english converter

feat.1.desc: Early modern english translator, middle english translator, Anglo-Saxon, and Simplified
feat.2.name: AI-Powered Accuracy

steps.title: How to Translate English to Old English in 3 Steps
steps.p: Learning how to translate english to old english is simple with our tool. Follow these three steps to translate from english to old english — no expertise required.
step.0.title: Choose a translation style from the dropdown
step.0.desc: Select Early Modern English (1500s Shakespeare style), Middle English (1300s Chaucer style), Anglo-Saxon Old English (800s Beowulf style), or Archaic Simplified.
step.1.title: Type or paste text into the input box
step.1.desc: Our old english text translator accepts up to 5,000 characters — roughly 800 words. Works for individual old english words or complete old english sentences.
step.2.title: Click Translate — copy the result
step.2.desc: Result appears instantly. Use History for past translations. Copy, download, or share your translated text.
middle.title: Middle English to Modern English Translator
middle.p1: Our tool doubles as a powerful middle english to modern english translator. Select the Middle English style, flip the direction with the ⇄ button, and paste your medieval text to translate middle english to modern english instantly. Whether you're deciphering Chaucer's Canterbury Tales or studying 14th-century manuscripts, our medieval english to modern english translator makes it effortless.
middle.p2: You can also use it as an english to middle english translator — simply type modern English and select the Medieval style to generate authentic Middle English output. This bidirectional capability makes it the most versatile middle english translator available online.
norse.title: Old Norse, Latin & Ancient Language Translators
norse.p: Interested in languages beyond Old English? Our platform is expanding to cover more ancient and historical languages:
norse.li0: Old Norse translator — Explore the Viking language that shaped modern English. Our guide on Viking and Old Norse influence on English covers key vocabulary, runes, and grammar. A dedicated english to old norse translator and old norse to english translator is coming soon.
norse.li1: Old Latin to English translator — Use our dedicated Latin Translator for Classical, Vulgar, and Medieval Latin conversion.
norse.li2: English translator — Browse all 30+ language translators including Arabic, Chinese, Greek, Spanish, and more.

faq.title: Old English Translator — Frequently Asked Questions
faq.0.q: What is the difference between Old English, Middle English, and Early Modern English?
faq.0.a: Old English (Anglo-Saxon, 800–1100 AD) is heavily Germanic and almost unrecognisable to modern readers — it is the language of Beowulf. Middle English (1100–1500 AD), as written by Chaucer, is more familiar but still requires a medieval english to modern english translator for most readers. Early Modern English (1500–1700 AD) includes Shakespeare's works and is the most readable archaic style. Our old english translator covers all three periods.
faq.1.q: Is this old english translator free to use?
faq.1.a: Yes — completely free. No account, no limits, no hidden fees. Our english to old english translator is free forever for personal and commercial use.
faq.2.a: Our old english translator uses context-aware AI — not word-for-word replacement. Whether you need to translate english to old english or convert middle english to modern english, results are authentic and natural-sounding for creative and educational purposes.
faq.3.q: Can I translate old language back to modern English?
faq.3.a: Yes. Use the ⇄ Flip button to switch direction. This works as a middle english to modern english translator, old english to modern english translator, or any supported style in reverse.
faq.4.q: What is the character limit for the old english text translator?
faq.4.a: Up to 5,000 characters per translation — approximately 800–1,000 words. This makes it suitable as both an old english sentence translator and a full old english text translator.
faq.5.q: Does the translator save my history?
faq.5.a: Yes. Your last 50 translations are saved locally in your browser only — nothing is sent to any server. This applies whether you use the english to old english translator, middle english translator, or any other style.
faq.6.q: Which translation style should I choose?
faq.6.a: Shakespeare (Early Modern) for creative writing, Medieval (Middle English) for historical fiction, Anglo-Saxon for academic use and authentic old english words translation, and Archaic Simplified for social media and casual fun.
faq.7.q: Can I use translations for commercial projects?
faq.7.a: Yes — no restrictions. Use the output from our old english translator in books, games, films, or any commercial project without attribution required.
faq.8.q: Can I translate English to Old Norse with this tool?
faq.8.a: Our tool specialises in Old English, Middle English, and Early Modern English translation. For Old Norse translation (English to Old Norse or Old Norse to English), we recommend exploring our guide on Viking and Old Norse influence on English, which covers key Old Norse vocabulary and phrases. A dedicated old norse translator is on our roadmap.
faq.9.q: How do I translate Middle English to modern English?
faq.9.a: To translate middle english to modern english, simply select the Middle English (1300s–1400s) style, click the ⇄ Flip button to switch to reverse mode, then paste your medieval english text and click Translate. Our medieval english to modern english translator handles Chaucerian-era texts with high accuracy.
faq.10.q: Does this tool translate Old Latin to English?
faq.10.a: For Old Latin to English translation, we recommend our dedicated Latin Translator tool, which handles Classical, Vulgar, and Medieval Latin. Our old english translator focuses specifically on the English language across its historical periods — Anglo-Saxon, Medieval, and Early Modern.
